sections.heading: Genetic
sections.content: A family history of RCC may indicate an inherited predisposition, such as a hereditary RCC syndrome [MS-5]. Several hereditary syndromes predispose to RCC, including von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease, hereditary leiomyomatosis and renal cell cancer (HLRCC), Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome, and others [MS-4, MS-32]. Almost exclusively associated with renal medullary carcinoma [MS-30].

sections.heading: Lifestyle
sections.content: Tobacco smoking is an established risk factor for RCC development [MS-4]. Obesity is an established risk factor for RCC development [MS-4].

sections.heading: Medical
sections.content: Hypertension is an established risk factor for RCC development [MS-4].

A family history of RCC may indicate an inherited predisposition, such as a hereditary RCC syndrome [MS-5].
Several hereditary syndromes predispose to RCC, including von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease, hereditary leiomyomatosis and renal cell cancer (HLRCC), Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome, and others [MS-4, MS-32].
Almost exclusively associated with renal medullary carcinoma [MS-30].
